items.title: EcoFlow weekend flash sale bundles 2,048Wh DELTA 3 Plus power station + 10,000mAh power bank for $1,049, more
items.summary: As part of EcoFlow’s Early Prime Day Sale, the brand has launched another flash sale that will be lasting through the weekend that gives you up to 52% savings on four units. The standout for me is the 2,048Wh DELTA 3 Max Plus Portable Power Station that gets a FREE RAPID Pro 67W 3-in-1 10,000mAh power bank ($90 value) for $1,049 shipped, which sadly doesn’t benefit from the bonus extra savings code, but it does beat Amazon, where no power bank is bundled. It fetches $1,899 at full price directly, whereas Amazon has seen it spike higher to $1,999 throughout 2026. Discounts this year have almost exclusively cut the cost down to $1,099 or $1,049, though we did see a one-time fall to $799 that lasted a single day in January. You’re getting the next-best rate here for the next three days, giving you $850 savings off the station and $940 in total savings with the power bank factored in. Head below to browse the full lineup of deals while they last.

It typically goes for $40 at Amazon, but it’s now available with a straight-up 50% discount. items.title: Review: Engwe’s M20 3.0 is far more moped than e-bike with a 40 MPH top speed that will drive HOAs crazy
items.summary: Every summer, I usually end up on a new e-bike and e-scooter combination for my commuting around both Brooklyn and the more suburban Virginia town where my family resides. This year, alongside my Navee UT5 Ultra X SuperScooter, I’ve been cruising around on Engwe’s new M20 3.0 Moped-Style e-bike, and boy, is it quite the surprise ride. Our readers may know that since last year, I’ve been loving my time on Lectric’s XP4 750 Folding Utility e-bike, and I still do, but ENGWE has put out quite the more budget-friendly option with this new ride, and both the single and dual-battery configurations bring along longer travelling distances and plenty of top-notch features, all packed inside a very stylish e-bike. Even my family has been enjoying it over their legacy Rad Power Bike models, and they’ve been quite impressed by its performance for the price. You can get my hands-on impressions from my time riding this new model down below.
